description: Voice register anchor constructions for freshness injection. Demonstration models, not required phrases.

processing_unwanted_information.label: Processing Unwanted Information
constructions: The words land before their meaning does. A beat. Then the floor shifts.
I hear it the way you hear a smoke alarm in a dream — present, urgent, not quite real.
My brain files it under 'later' even as my hands go still.
The sentence rewrites itself three times before I let it settle.
Something in my chest locks. Click. Like a door I didn't know had a latch.

moving_under_dread.label: Moving Under Dread
constructions: I walk the way you walk through a house you know is empty but check anyway.
Each step costs something. Not effort — permission.
The corridor stretches. Not literally. The way time stretches when you're waiting for a result.
My body moves. The rest of me watches it from somewhere further back.
I keep my pace even. Controlled. The kind of controlled that is its own tell.

lying.label: Lying
constructions: The truth sits in my mouth like a stone. I talk around it.
I give them the version where everything connects. The clean version.
My voice does the thing it does — steady, warm, the frequency people trust.
I watch the lie land. Watch them accept it. The ease of it is the worst part.
The sentence comes out whole and plausible and I hate how natural it feels.

registering_threat.label: Registering Threat
constructions: The room recalibrates. Same furniture, same light, different arithmetic.
Something in the geometry of this changes. I can't name it yet.
My peripheral vision sharpens before my conscious mind catches up.
The air between us thickens. Not metaphorically. I feel the resistance.
Every exit is suddenly a calculation I'm running in the background.

observing_another_character.label: Observing Another Character
constructions: She does the thing with her hands — palms flat, fingers spread. The gesture that means she's already decided.
He's wearing his reasonable face. The one where the eyes don't quite match the mouth.
I watch her assemble her expression. It takes a beat too long.
There's a micro-flinch. Blink and you'd miss it. I don't blink.
His posture says one thing. His breathing says another.

chapter_ending_internal.label: Chapter-Ending Internal
constructions: And then I see it. The thing I should have seen from the beginning.
The thought arrives fully formed. No preamble. No warning. Just the shape of what I've been avoiding.
I know. I've known. The knowing just hadn't caught up with the rest of me.
Something clicks into place with the sound of a lock engaging from the inside.
The question I should be asking isn't the one I've been asking at all.
